title: Prior Art Entry
description: Single prior-art block. The prior-art library is a list of these. The library serves two purposes: (1) authoring drop-in prose blocks for the spec's Background section, and (2) maintaining a portfolio-wide ground-truth of what's been considered (defensive completeness for prosecution).

citation.description: Full canonical citation as it would appear in a spec or prosecution document.

relevance.description: Why this prior art matters to the portfolio. Specifically: what does it teach, and how does the present invention distinguish from it?

distinctionFromInvention.description: The specific structural or methodological gap this prior art has that the invention fills. Used both for spec drafting and for prosecution arguments.

blockProse.description: Drop-in prose block for use in spec Background sections. Plain text; rendered into spec at build time. Optional — prior-art entries can be reference-only without block prose.

blockSection.description: Section label in the prior-art library document (e.g., '§1.2 Patent landscape', '§2A Sensor positioning').

status.description: candidate = found in research, not yet evaluated. evaluated = read and assessed, may or may not be cited. cited = appears in at least one spec. rejected = evaluated and intentionally not cited (with rationale).
